skill.name: agentation
skill.description: Adds the Agentation visual feedback toolbar to a Next.js project.
skill.summary: Guides installation and setup of Agentation in Next.js apps.
skill.author: benjitaylor

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backtick, environment access, sensitive file, and weak cryptography patterns. Review found Markdown formatting false positives and safe NODE_ENV checks, but line 14 does instruct installing an external npm package. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network calls, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: External Dependency Installation Instruction
medium_findings.description: Line 14 instructs the assistant to run npm install for the agentation package if it is missing. This is a legitimate setup step, but it modifies project dependencies and introduces third-party package supply-chain risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The install command is explicit and would change the user's dependency tree. The skill intent is legitimate, so the risk is elevated but not malicious.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown inline code and TSX setup examples. No evidence found that these lines execute Ruby backticks or shell substitution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown, and the flagged text appears inside bullets or fenced TSX examples. There is no executable Ruby or shell context.

low_findings.title: Environment Access Is Development Mode Guard
low_findings.description: The process.env.NODE_ENV references only conditionally render Agentation in development. They do not access secrets, read environment files, or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both references are fixed NODE_ENV checks in visible setup snippets. No secret-bearing environment variable is referenced.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Has No Evidence
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography at line 3, but that line is the frontmatter description. No cryptographic algorithm appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains only descriptive metadata. There is no hash, cipher, or cryptographic operation to review.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party Package Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup flow can run npm install agentation. Users should review the package source, version, and lockfile changes before accepting installation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The package installation instruction is explicit. It is a common legitimate pattern, but third-party installs carry supply-chain risk.

content.value_statement: Next.js teams need a quick way to add visual feedback tools during development. This skill checks the project structure, installs Agentation, and adds the toolbar in the right entry point.
actual_capabilities: Checks package dependencies for an existing Agentation install.
Installs the agentation npm package when it is missing.
Detects Next.js App Router and Pages Router layouts.
Adds the Agentation component to the appropriate root file.
Wraps the toolbar with a development-only NODE_ENV guard.
limitations: Supports Next.js projects, not other React frameworks.
Requires React 18 according to the skill notes.
Does not configure authentication, storage, or custom Agentation options.
Requires the user to run the development server to confirm the toolbar.

use_cases.title: Add Feedback During Feature Work
use_cases.description: Install Agentation in a local Next.js app so reviewers can mark interface issues during development.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Product Review Build
use_cases.description: Add a development-only feedback toolbar before showing a feature branch to product stakeholders.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Setup
use_cases.description: Apply a consistent Agentation setup across App Router and Pages Router projects.

prompt_templates.title: Install Agentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup in a Next.js project
prompt_templates.prompt: Add Agentation to this Next.js project. Check whether it is already installed before changing files.

prompt_templates.title: Configure the Correct Router
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose App Router or Pages Router setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Detect whether this project uses the App Router or Pages Router, then add Agentation in the correct root file.

prompt_templates.title: Review Before Installing
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether Agentation is already present. If installation is needed, explain the dependency change before you install it.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Existing Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this project for an existing Agentation setup, confirm it only loads in development, and fix any duplicate or misplaced toolbar usage.

output_examples.input: Add Agentation to my App Router project.
output_examples.output: The assistant reports that it found an App Router layout, added Agentation after the page content, and limited it to development mode.

output_examples.input: Check whether Agentation is already installed.
output_examples.output: The assistant reports whether the dependency and component import already exist, then stops without making duplicate changes.

output_examples.input: Set up Agentation but avoid production loading.
output_examples.output: The assistant adds the toolbar behind a development environment check and asks the user to confirm it in the local dev server.

best_practices: Review package and lockfile changes before accepting installation.
Keep the Agentation component behind a development-only environment check.
Run the Next.js dev server after setup to confirm the toolbar appears.
anti_patterns: Do not add Agentation to production-only render paths.
Do not install the package twice or add duplicate toolbar components.
Do not skip router detection before editing root application files.

faq.question: What does this skill install?
faq.answer: It installs the agentation npm package when the project does not already include it.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it support?
faq.answer: It is written for Next.js projects using either App Router or Pages Router.

faq.question: Does it load in production?
faq.answer: The setup examples wrap Agentation with a NODE_ENV development check.

faq.question: Does it require extra configuration?
faq.answer: The skill notes say no additional configuration is required for the default setup.

faq.question: Can it detect an existing setup?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks for the package and existing Agentation component usage before adding changes.

faq.question: What should I review before using it?
faq.answer: Review dependency changes, edited root files, and whether the toolbar is limited to development.
